articles.title: The AI Memory System
articles.summary: You'll build: a persistent memory system that compounds intelligence over time Most people start every AI conversation from scratch. The top 1% build knowledge bases, decision logs, and self-auditing loops that make every session better than the last.

articles.title: The Error Budget
articles.summary: You'll build: a reliability framework with error taxonomy, budgets, and graceful degradation Zero errors is not a realistic goal for AI systems. Build the framework that defines acceptable failure rates, measures them in real time, and degrades gracefully when limits are hit.

articles.title: The Memory Palace
articles.summary: You'll build: a retrieval-augmented memory system for long-running AI projects Long AI projects break when context gets too large to fit in a single window. Build the architecture that stores, indexes, and retrieves exactly the right memory for each task.

articles.title: The Onboarding Architect
articles.summary: You'll build: an AI-powered onboarding system that adapts to each new hire Generic onboarding fails because every new hire has different gaps. Build the system that identifies what each person needs to know, generates a personalized learning path, and validates retention.

articles.title: The Competitive Map
articles.summary: You'll build: a living competitive intelligence system that updates itself Most competitive analysis is a snapshot that goes stale in 60 days. Build the structured framework that maps competitors by capability, surfaces positioning gaps, and flags new entrants before they matter.

articles.title: The Objection Handler
articles.summary: You'll build: a living objection playbook trained on your real customer conversations Every sales team has the same 8 objections and 8 inconsistent answers. Build the system that synthesizes your best responses, detects patterns, and generates the reply that actually converts.

articles.title: The Meeting Extractor
articles.summary: You'll build: a post-meeting system that turns notes into decisions, actions, and follow-ups Most meetings end with vague notes and unclear ownership. Build the extraction system that converts any meeting transcript into a decision log, action list, and follow-up draft in minutes.

articles.title: The Research Stack
articles.summary: You'll build: a three-layer research process from broad to deep Most AI research stops at the surface. These prompts build a structured stack: landscape scan, deep dive, and synthesis — so you arrive at insight, not just information.

articles.title: The Proposal Machine
articles.summary: You'll build: a repeatable system for generating winning proposals in under an hour Most proposals fail before they are read because they describe your capabilities, not the client's problem. Build the template that forces customer-first framing every time.

articles.title: The Data Analyst
articles.summary: You'll build: a structured analysis framework for any business dataset Raw data tells you what happened. These prompts help Claude tell you why it happened, what to do about it, and what to watch for next.

articles.title: The Audience Switch
articles.summary: You'll build: a communication translation system for any audience The same message lands completely differently with a CFO vs. an engineer vs. a customer. Build the framework that rewrites any content for any audience without losing accuracy.

articles.title: The Devil's Advocate
articles.summary: You'll build: a structured process for stress-testing any plan or decision Your AI will agree with you by default. These three prompts force it to find the flaws in your reasoning before you commit to an expensive mistake.

articles.title: The Context Window
articles.summary: You'll build: a context loading strategy that maximizes every token Most prompts waste 40% of context on the wrong information. Build the architecture that loads exactly what the task needs — no more, no less.

articles.title: The Feedback Engine
articles.summary: You'll build: a system that turns user feedback into automatic prompt improvements Every negative user reaction is a signal. Build the loop that collects those signals, classifies them by failure type, and generates prompt revisions automatically.

articles.title: The Control Room
articles.summary: You'll build: a unified monitoring interface for your entire AI system You built agents, quality gates, and an orchestra. But where do you see all of it at once? Build four monitoring layers that give you complete system health in 90 seconds.

articles.title: The Orchestra
articles.summary: You'll build: a conductor layer that coordinates multi-agent workflows Individual agents are reliable. The system of agents is fragile. Build the conductor that manages dependencies, sequencing, and handoffs between agents.

articles.title: The Safety Net
articles.summary: You'll build: an incident response system that turns every agent failure into a permanent fix Your agents run autonomously. But what happens when one fails at 2 AM? Build a three-layer safety net — detection, escalation, and post-mortem generation.

articles.title: The Handoff
articles.summary: You'll build: an earned-autonomy framework that lets your best agents run themselves Your metrics prove which agents are reliable. Build a three-tier system where agents graduate from review-required to autonomous based on their accuracy track record.

articles.title: The Feedback Loop
articles.summary: You'll build: a metrics layer that makes your AI system improve itself weekly Your agents run daily. But are they getting better? Build a metrics layer that tracks accuracy, detects drift, and tells you exactly what to fix next.

articles.title: The Quality Gate
articles.summary: You'll build: an AI review loop that catches agent errors before you do Your agents will make mistakes. Build a review layer that catches errors, hallucinations, and stale data before they reach your dashboard.

articles.title: The Decision Dashboard
articles.summary: You'll build: one morning view that aggregates all your agents' output You built the agents. Now build the dashboard that makes their output actionable in 5 minutes.

articles.title: The Persistent Agent
articles.summary: You'll build: an AI system that runs every morning without you Issue #10 showed you how to run one overnight task. This issue shows you how to build a system that never stops working.

articles.title: The Research Synthesizer
articles.summary: You'll build: 15 tabs into one clear briefing You already have all the information. The problem is it is scattered across twelve different places.

articles.title: The Negotiation Brief
articles.summary: You'll build: a pre-negotiation preparation brief Most people negotiate on instinct. Here is how to negotiate on preparation.

articles.title: The Overnight Run
articles.summary: You'll build: an AI loop that runs while you sleep Most people use AI reactively — question, answer, repeat. Here is how to build a system that runs on its own.

articles.title: The Reasoning Audit
articles.summary: You'll build: a 10-min thinking pressure test Most thinking errors are visible — if you know how to look. Here is how to use AI to find them before they cost you.

articles.title: The Decision Log
articles.summary: You'll build: a system that captures why you decided Most decisions disappear the moment you move on. Here is how to capture them so future-you can learn from them.

articles.title: Persistent AI Context
articles.summary: You'll build: a context file AI remembers forever Every new conversation starts from zero. Unless you build the system that prevents it.

articles.title: The Data Analyst
articles.summary: You'll build: data-to-recommendation in 5 minutes Most data sits in spreadsheets nobody looks at. Here is how to make it speak.

articles.title: The Contract Decoder
articles.summary: You'll build: a 10-min contract review workflow You have signed documents you did not fully read. Here is how to fix that going forward.

articles.title: The Email Drafter
articles.summary: You'll build: emails that close loops, not create them The problem is not how fast you write. It is that your emails create more work than they resolve.

articles.title: The Meeting Prep
articles.summary: You'll build: a 15-min meeting simulation Most people prepare by reviewing the agenda. You are going to prepare by simulating the conversation.

articles.title: The Weekly Review
articles.summary: You'll build: a 10-minute Friday review system Most people end their week without processing what happened. This changes that.

articles.title: ★ Start Here The Briefing Document
articles.summary: You'll build: a 1-page brief from any research pile Most AI output is too long, too generic, and too boring. Here is the fix.

articles.title: Anthropic built a model too risky to release
articles.summary: and Meta makes an unexpected entry

articles.title: No Claude for Claws
articles.summary: in 3 months, Anthropic added $6B ARR
